worker_launch.note: All 5 required leaf workers were launched exactly once. Synthesis supplements failed worker scopes with direct code evidence.

what_exists: PID liveness detection via os.kill(pid, 0) in orchestrator polling loop
tmux pane census (pane_dead + pane_current_command) in monitor
stale PID cleanup after dead-worker detection

evidence.detail: PID heartbeat and dead worker collection
dead-worker recovery loop + stale pid removal

risks: PID reuse can produce false liveness and delay reclaim
Detection split across orchestrator and monitor creates divergent behavior

what_exists: Dead-worker reclaim by matching claimed_by, incrementing generation, requeueing or failing after max_generation=2
Orphan sweep requeues stale claims older than 600s
Monitor fallback requeues stale claims when no live agents for >=180s
Resume flow requeues all claimed tasks after crash

evidence.detail: claim + atomic result write + claimed cleanup

risks: SKILL reclaim uses write-then-delete (non-atomic move), allowing duplicate presence on crash
Monitor reclaim path does not enforce generation cap or metadata clearing
Monitor reclaim only runs when live_agents==0, so single-worker death may stall until orchestrator/orphan sweep

what_exists: Orphan sweep by claim file mtime > 600s
Monitor stale-claim reclaim after 180s with commander heartbeat guard
Teardown removes PID files and kills session-specific worker PIDs
risks: No persistent monitor recovery state; monitor restart resets dead-window timer
No explicit cleanup for orphan .tmp result files beyond filename exclusion logic

what_docs_cover: README states dead-agent PID detection and re-queue behavior
Skill command table includes status/resume/teardown flows
Crash recovery step documents requeue of claimed tasks
gaps: Docs do not clearly explain dual reclaim authorities (orchestrator + monitor) and precedence
Docs claim atomic patterns broadly but reclaim path in SKILL is not atomic rename
No explicit operator runbook for partial-worker death while others remain live

high_priority_cases.impact: Claim can lack claimed_by if worker dies after rename and before rewrite; dead-worker reclaim miss
high_priority_cases.evidence: agents/stampede-agent.agent.md:130-136 with SKILL claimed_by match at 506

high_priority_cases.impact: Crash between queue write and claimed delete can duplicate task across queue/claimed
Monitor and orchestrator can both reclaim same claim with different semantics
SKILL dead-worker reclaim lacks results existence guard; may requeue already-completed task
high_priority_cases.evidence: skills/SKILL.md:500-521 (no result check), monitor has guard at 274

high_priority_cases.impact: Dead worker may appear alive, delaying recovery

recommended_actions: Unify reclaim implementation to single atomic rename path (claimed -> queue) shared by monitor and orchestrator
Always check for existing results/{task}.json before any requeue action
Persist monitor recovery state (e.g., all-dead-since) in run dir to survive monitor restarts
Augment PID checks with process start-time or pane-bound identity validation
Document precise recovery authority and failure-mode runbooks (single worker death, monitor restart, dual-reclaimer behavior)

overall_assessment.summary: Recovery mechanisms are substantial (dead detection, reclaim, orphan sweep, resume), but split control paths and non-atomic requeue in the SKILL path introduce correctness risk under crashes and races.
